meta.importedFrom: Unit 5 Passage 1 Secrets caught in time（互动课文教学动画）英语新教材外研版八年级下册.html

article.title: Secrets caught in time
article.summary: 在被时间保存下来的线索里，观察过去、记忆和变化。

sentences.text: Imagine a dinosaur in an ancient forest.
sentences.tr: 想象一下远古森林里的一只恐龙。
sentences.d: 祈使句，以动词原形Imagine开头。ancient意为“远古的”。

sentences.text: It is running around looking for its next meal.
sentences.tr: 它正跑来跑去寻找下一顿饭。
sentences.d: is running表示正在进行的动作。looking for是现在分词短语作伴随状语。

sentences.text: With an angry cry, it opens its mouth, raises its beautiful feather-covered arms, and…
sentences.tr: 伴随着一声愤怒的叫喊，它张开嘴，举起它那覆盖着美丽羽毛的手臂，然后……
sentences.d: With...是介词短语作状语。feather-covered是复合形容词。

sentences.text: Wait, feathers on a dinosaur?
sentences.tr: 等等，恐龙身上有羽毛？
sentences.d: 省略句，表达惊讶。feathers on a dinosaur意为“恐龙身上的羽毛”。

sentences.text: For hundreds of years, people believed that dinosaurs looked like huge lizards.
sentences.tr: 几百年来，人们一直认为恐龙看起来像巨大的蜥蜴。
sentences.d: that引导宾语从句。look like意为“看起来像”。

sentences.text: Just look at all those dinosaurs in books, films and TV shows!
sentences.tr: 看看书、电影和电视节目里的那些恐龙吧！
sentences.d: look at意为“看”。

sentences.text: However, thanks to a piece of amber, scientists now know that this idea is wrong.
sentences.tr: 然而，多亏了一块琥珀，科学家们现在知道这个想法是错误的。
sentences.d: thanks to意为“多亏了”。that引导宾语从句。

sentences.text: In the amber from Southeast Asia, they found the tail of a dinosaur – with feathers.
sentences.tr: 在来自东南亚的琥珀中，他们发现了一条恐龙的尾巴——带有羽毛。
sentences.d: from Southeast Asia是定语修饰amber。with feathers补充说明尾巴的特征。

sentences.text: These beautiful golden stones quietly keep pieces of the past.
sentences.tr: 这些美丽的金色石头静静地保存着过去的碎片。
sentences.d: golden stones指代琥珀。pieces of the past意为“过去的碎片”。

sentences.text: They can tell us whether dinosaurs had feathers, but that’s not all.
sentences.tr: 它们可以告诉我们恐龙是否有羽毛，但这还不是全部。
sentences.d: whether引导宾语从句，意为“是否”。

sentences.text: They also help us understand that life on earth has always changed.
sentences.tr: 它们还帮助我们理解地球上的生命一直在变化。
sentences.d: help sb. do sth. 帮助某人做某事。that引导宾语从句。

sentences.text: A piece of amber from Northern Europe, for example, holds a flower.
sentences.tr: 例如，一块来自北欧的琥珀里有一朵花。
sentences.d: for example是插入语，意为“例如”。

sentences.text: This species no longer grows in this area.
sentences.tr: 这个物种不再在这个地区生长。
sentences.d: no longer意为“不再”。

sentences.text: But the find proves that it did 35 million years ago.
sentences.tr: 但这一发现证明了它在3500万年前确实生长在这里。
sentences.d: did在此处是助动词，代替grows，起强调作用。

sentences.text: In the future, amber may also help us discover if other species have changed over time.
sentences.tr: 在未来，琥珀也可能帮助我们发现其他物种是否随着时间的推移而发生了变化。
sentences.d: if引导宾语从句，意为“是否”。over time意为“随着时间的推移”。

sentences.text: Thanks to amber, more than 300 unknown species have been discovered.
sentences.tr: 多亏了琥珀，300多种未知的物种被发现了。
sentences.d: have been discovered是现在完成时的被动语态。

sentences.text: In a piece of amber from Africa, scientists found 13 ants.
sentences.tr: 在来自非洲的一块琥珀中，科学家发现了13只蚂蚁。
sentences.d: from Africa是定语修饰amber。

sentences.text: Research shows that they are a completely new species.
sentences.tr: 研究表明它们是一个全新的物种。
sentences.d: that引导宾语从句。completely是副词修饰形容词new。

sentences.text: Scientists used to believe that ants came from the northern half of the earth.
sentences.tr: 科学家们过去认为蚂蚁来自地球的北半球。
sentences.d: used to do sth. 过去常常做某事。northern half意为“北半部”。

sentences.text: The discovery of this African species has changed old beliefs about the history of ants.
sentences.tr: 这一非洲物种的发现改变了关于蚂蚁历史的旧观念。
sentences.d: has changed是现在完成时。beliefs意为“信念，观念”。

sentences.text: Who knows whether this piece of amber will tell us more secrets?
sentences.tr: 谁知道这块琥珀是否会告诉我们更多的秘密？
sentences.d: whether引导宾语从句。Who knows意为“谁知道呢”。

sentences.text: As a window on the past, amber has been much studied.
sentences.tr: 作为了解过去的一扇窗户，琥珀已经被广泛研究。
sentences.d: As...意为“作为...”。has been studied是现在完成时的被动语态。

sentences.text: However, it’s still not fully understood.
sentences.tr: 然而，它仍然没有被完全理解。
sentences.d: it's 是 it is 的缩写。fully意为“完全地”。

sentences.text: We should put more effort into research.
sentences.tr: 我们应该在研究上投入更多的精力。
sentences.d: should意为“应该”。put effort into意为“在...投入精力”。

sentences.text: Then we can use this key to unlock more secrets of nature.
sentences.tr: 然后我们就可以用这把钥匙开启更多大自然的秘密。
sentences.d: use... to do... 用...去做...。unlock意为“开启，解锁”。

vocabItems.meaningZh: 这；那 (在元音前特殊发音)
有；吃；持有 (have的第三人称单数)
是 (be的第二人称单复数、第一三人称复数)
改变 (change的过去式/过去分词)
持有；包含 (hold的第三人称单数)
发现 (discover的过去式/过去分词)
研究 (study的过去式/过去分词)
理解 (understand的过去式/过去分词)

readingQuestions.stem: What did people use to believe about dinosaurs?
options: A. They had feathers
B. They looked like huge lizards
C. They lived in amber
D. They were small animals
readingQuestions.answer: B. They looked like huge lizards
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 第二段提到：For hundreds of years, people believed that dinosaurs looked like huge lizards.

readingQuestions.stem: What was found in the amber from Southeast Asia?
options: A. A whole dinosaur
B. A dinosaur's tail with feathers
C. A flower
D. 13 ants
readingQuestions.answer: B. A dinosaur's tail with feathers
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 第二段结尾提到：In the amber from Southeast Asia, they found the tail of a dinosaur – with feathers.

readingQuestions.stem: What does the flower in the amber from Northern Europe prove?
options: A. Flowers no longer grow on earth
B. This species grew there 35 million years ago
C. Amber can create new flowers
D. Northern Europe was once a desert
readingQuestions.answer: B. This species grew there 35 million years ago
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 第三段提到：But the find proves that it did 35 million years ago.

readingQuestions.stem: Where did scientists use to believe ants came from?
options: A. Africa
B. Southeast Asia
C. The northern half of the earth
D. Northern Europe
readingQuestions.answer: C. The northern half of the earth
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 第四段提到：Scientists used to believe that ants came from the northern half of the earth.

readingQuestions.stem: Why does the author call amber a 'window on the past'?
options: A. Because it is transparent like glass
B. Because it helps us see and understand the past
C. Because it is found near windows
D. Because it is very expensive
readingQuestions.answer: B. Because it helps us see and understand the past
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 全文描述了琥珀如何保存过去的生物和信息，帮助我们了解历史，因此它是了解过去的窗户。

the_vowel_special.meaningZh: 这；那 (在元音前特殊发音)

has.meaningZh: 有；吃；持有 (have的第三人称单数)

are.meaningZh: 是 (be的第二人称单复数、第一三人称复数)

changed.meaningZh: 改变 (change的过去式/过去分词)

holds.meaningZh: 持有；包含 (hold的第三人称单数)

discovered.meaningZh: 发现 (discover的过去式/过去分词)

studied.meaningZh: 研究 (study的过去式/过去分词)

understood.meaningZh: 理解 (understand的过去式/过去分词)
